title: The Archive
tagline: 2,800 years of records. Every result the same.
hook_line: ELDER-7 has carried that data pad for 2,800 years. Tonight he opens it fully for the first time.

scenes.title: The Pad He Carries
scenes.narration: ELDER-7 has carried that data pad for 2,800 years. He has never opened it fully for any team before. Tonight he does.
scenes.visual_prompt: Close-up of ELDER-7's ancient bronze hand holding the data pad, aged and worn surface, amber glow from the screen beginning to intensify as it opens, the scale of 40 meters of ancient mech visible in background, dark cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: These Are Your Predecessors
scenes.narration: 'These are your predecessors,' he said. 'Every team that faced what you are facing now. Every record I have kept.'
scenes.visual_prompt: ELDER-7 holding the data pad outward toward the gathered team — BLAZE GRANITE NOVA STORM RUMBLE — all illuminated by the amber glow of the archive, ELDER-7 at his observing angle never fully forward, faces of the team readable, weight of what they're about to see, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Entry by Entry
scenes.narration: TITAN-FOUR. Charged alone. FALLEN. ATLAS-9. Mapped everything. FALLEN. SHADOW-1. Fastest mech in recorded history. FALLEN.
scenes.visual_prompt: The data pad's archive display scrolling slowly, entries appearing one by one in amber text on dark screen — TITAN-FOUR: FALLEN, ATLAS-9: FALLEN, SHADOW-1: FALLEN — each with battle record dates spanning centuries, the red designation of FALLEN repeating, cinematic close-up, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: SIEGE-3
scenes.narration: SIEGE-3. Held for eleven hours. FALLEN. IRON-TWELVE. Largest mech ever deployed against a conqueror. FALLEN. VANGUARD. FALLEN.
scenes.visual_prompt: Archive scrolling continues, SIEGE-3 entry showing 11 hour battle record then FALLEN, IRON-TWELVE with massive scale notation then FALLEN, VANGUARD entry brief then FALLEN, the pattern relentless and damning, amber text on ancient dark display, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Forty-Seven
scenes.narration: Forty-seven entries. Some held for days. Some broke through his lines. Some reached his command. Every result the same. FALLEN.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot of the data pad with the full archive visible — 47 entries scrolling as a cascade, each with different battle records and circumstances, all ending in the same red designation: FALLEN, FALLEN, FALLEN — a wall of failure across 2800 years, amber on black, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: All of Them
scenes.narration: RUMBLE, very quietly: 'How many of them thought they'd be different?' ELDER-7, without hesitation: 'All of them.'
scenes.visual_prompt: RUMBLE crimson red and black bruiser mech, 28 meters, massive but strangely still in this moment, looking at the archive display, ELDER-7 answering without looking at him — always at his observing angle — the weight of that answer visible in the silence between them, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: What Is Different
scenes.narration: NOVA: 'What's different about us?' ELDER-7 is quiet for a long time. The team waits. He doesn't answer. Not yet.
scenes.visual_prompt: NOVA cold cyan blue close-up, her visor showing the archive reflection in miniature, the question hanging in the air, ELDER-7 visible behind her at his eternal observing angle, the amber glow of the data pad between them, ELDER-7's expression unreadable but something is there — a waiting, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Blank Field
scenes.narration: He scrolled to the final entry. TEAM SIX. The date. Their battle record so far. And then — the result field. Blank.
scenes.visual_prompt: Data pad close-up, final archive entry displayed: TEAM SIX, current date timestamp, battle engagements listed, and then the result field completely empty — a blank space where 47 entries before it all read FALLEN — the blankness more powerful than any text, amber display, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: BLAZE Reads It
scenes.narration: BLAZE looked at the blank field. Then at the forty-seven FALLEN entries above it. Something was forming. He didn't say it yet.
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE hot orange mech close-up, amber visor reflecting the archive display, his expression visibly processing — not fear, not despair, but calculation, something building, the 47 FALLEN entries visible in the reflection, the blank result field at the bottom, cinematic intimate, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: When He Is Done Learning
scenes.narration: ELDER-7 closed the pad. 'Tomorrow he advances. With everything. This is what he does when he has finished learning what he faces.'
scenes.visual_prompt: ELDER-7 closing the data pad with slow deliberate motion, amber glow disappearing as it closes, the team left in the near-dark of the night, distant horizon showing the vast illuminated encampment of Xerxes's army, the scale of what is coming visible in that distance, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

viral_hook: Forty-seven teams before them. Every result the same. FALLEN. FALLEN. FALLEN. And then their entry — blank.
highlight_note: The blank result field. After 47 FALLEN entries, the blank space carries more weight than any word could. Viewers feel what BLAZE feels.
rewatch_bait: BLAZE staring at the blank field in scene 9 — rewatch EP012 and come back. What he's thinking in that moment is exactly what he says in EP010.
youtube_title: 47 Teams Came Before Them. Every Single One: FALLEN. | Mech Legends S1 EP007
lesson: History doesn't mean you'll repeat it. But you have to actually read it differently.
arc_notes: EP007 is the pivot of the season. Everything before was setup. Everything after is consequence. BLAZE's 'something forming' beat in scene 9 is the seed of EP010's revelation. ELDER-7 showing the archive is his most vulnerable moment — he's trusted them with 2800 years of record.
